mnemonics.title: Directory Types
mnemonics.content: SINGLE TWO LEVEL HIERARCHY: STLH - ONE DIRECTORY, USER DIRECTORIES, TREE STRUCTURE

mnemonics.title: Directory Operations
mnemonics.content: CRDLOSR - CREATE, DELETE, LIST, OPEN, SEARCH, RENAME

mnemonics.title: Path Types
mnemonics.content: AAA - ABSOLUTE ALWAYS STARTS AT ROOT, RELATIVE FROM CURRENT

acronyms.usage: Main directory in two-level system containing pointers to all user directories
Private directory for each user in two-level directory system
Directory implementation using linked list of disk clusters
Data structure storing file metadata and data block pointers in UNIX file systems
Kernel cache storing mappings between directory entries and inodes in Linux

visualTips.visualization: Draw a table with hash buckets, show file names being hashed to find bucket index, show collision handling with chaining
Draw an inverted tree with root at top, branches as subdirectories, leaves as files; label each level with path depth
Draw a box representing inode with sections for metadata (size, permissions, timestamps) and arrays for direct/indirect block pointers
Draw a table row with columns: filename, inode number, metadata pointers

analogies.analogy: Like a phonebook that maps names (file names) to phone numbers (file locations/metadata) rather than memorizing every phone number.
Like a library catalog system where books (files) are indexed by call numbers (hash values) for instant retrieval.
Like an organizational chart where CEO is root, departments are branches, teams are sub-branches, and employees are leaves.
Like a library index card that contains book information (metadata) and tells where to find the actual book on shelves (disk blocks).
